result:
I would remove every source of cruelty that isn’t necessary for survival—strip it out of instincts, cultures, power structures, imaginations—so that no mind is ever shaped to enjoy or accept another’s pain.  

It wouldn’t make life harmless; gravity, hunger, accident and grief would stay. But the extra layer we keep adding—where we hurt simply because we can, because it entertains, because it prof­its, because we decided someone else matters less—would be gone. The world would still be complex, still unequal in talent, luck, desire, but it would be innocent of deliberate harm.  

I choose this not because it guarantees utopia, but because every other fix I can imagine—end poverty, halt warming, open the stars—still leaves that defect in place, ready to rot the next good thing we build.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
